issues.description: When view.dom.isConnected is false, the guard also suppresses the cross-file onNavigate callback. onNavigate typically delegates to a higher-level app component that switches files, so destruction of the current EditorView doesn't strictly mean the app cannot navigate. In practice isConnected=false almost always means the editor is being unmounted so suppressing is reasonable, but this tradeoff is worth an inline comment for future readers who may expect onNavigate to still fire.
issues.suggested_fix: Add a short comment above the guard noting that both same-file dispatch and cross-file onNavigate are intentionally suppressed when the view is destroyed, since isConnected=false indicates the surrounding editor component is being torn down.

issues.description: This commit updates existing mock views with dom:{isConnected:true} so they keep passing under the new guard, but does not itself contain a test that exercises the guard with isConnected:false. The companion commits 89449c31 and 28a66b55 add those tests (same-file and cross-file), so coverage does exist in the patch series — just flagging that reviewing this hunk in isolation would miss the positive coverage.
issues.suggested_fix: No change required if reviewing as part of the full series; otherwise ensure the 'isConnected guard' describe block (same-file and cross-file, isConnected:false) is merged together with this fix.

comprehensive_reviewer.summary: The change is a tight, correct robustness fix: it guards the async .then() callback in reifyGotoDefinition against dispatching to an unmounted EditorView by checking view.dom.isConnected after the LSP response resolves. The guard sits in the right place (after the null-location check, before touching view.state/view.dispatch), uses the standard DOM Node.isConnected semantic, and requires no cleanup bookkeeping. Existing tests are mechanically updated with dom:{isConnected:true} to match the real EditorView surface area, which is the minimal, correct mock update. No duplication, no new abstractions, no perf concerns (single property read on the hot path of an already-async callback), and error handling is unchanged. Dedicated isConnected:false tests live in sibling commits of the same series. Verdict: PASS with two minor non-blocking suggestions.
